---
title: "figure5_network"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "11/18/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)
library(Hmisc)
library(writexl)

library(tidygraph)

```


#demographic
```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862_new.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
species = read_xlsx("data/species_866.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")

food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(across(c("Mushrooms","Chapati"), as.numeric))
food_data
nutrition_data
species
metadata
```

```{r}
colnames(species)[1] = "Participant_ID"
colnames(metadata)[1] = "Participant_ID"
threshold <- nrow(species) * 0.5

species_filtered1 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > nrow(species) * 0.2)
species_filtered1
species_filtered2 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)

#abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
#species_filtered2 <- species_filtered %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(species)))
#species_filtered2
species_filtered2 <- species_filtered2[, !names(species_filtered2) %in% c("unclassified", "others", "Homo sapiens")]
#species_filtered2$`Homo sapiens`
#species_filtered2
#food_data[is.na(food_data)] <- 0
#food_data <- data.frame(lapply(food_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
nutrition_data[is.na(nutrition_data)] <- 0
```


```{r}
food_data
nutrition_data
species_filtered2
species_filtered1
metadata
```


## knowledge graph
```{r}
create_microbiome_knowledge_graph <- function(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                            species_data, metadata,
                                            species_threshold = 0.3) {  # Add threshold parameter
  # Ensure all datasets have the same participants and in the same order
  common_participants <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    food_data$Participant_ID,
    nutrition_data$Participant_ID,
    species_data$Participant_ID
  ))
  
  # Filter and arrange all datasets
  food_data <- food_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  nutrition_data <- nutrition_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  species_data <- species_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  # Get categories
  food_categories <- setdiff(colnames(food_data), "Participant_ID")
  nutrition_categories <- setdiff(colnames(nutrition_data), "Participant_ID")
  species_categories <- setdiff(colnames(species_data), "Participant_ID")
  
  # Calculate correlations with p-values using rcorr
  calculate_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    complete_cases <- complete.cases(mat1, mat2)
    if(!all(complete_cases)) {
      mat1 <- mat1[complete_cases, ]
      mat2 <- mat2[complete_cases, ]
    }
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(cbind(mat1, mat2), type = "spearman")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  species_food_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  species_nutrition_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  # Create edges from correlations
  create_correlation_edges <- function(cor_results, relationship_type) {
    cors <- cor_results$correlations
    p_values <- cor_results$p_values
    edges <- which(p_values <= 0.01 & abs(cors) >= species_threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)  # Add threshold filter
    
    if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
      tibble(
        from = rownames(cors)[edges[,1]],
        to = colnames(cors)[edges[,2]],
        relationship = relationship_type,
        weight = cors[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])],
        p_value = p_values[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])]
      )
    } else {
      tibble(from = character(), to = character(), 
             relationship = character(), weight = numeric(),
             p_value = numeric())
    }
  }
  
  # Get species that have significant strong connections
  species_with_connections <- unique(c(
    rownames(species_food_results$correlations)[rowSums(species_food_results$p_values <= 0.01 & 
                                                        abs(species_food_results$correlations) >= species_threshold) > 0],
    rownames(species_nutrition_results$correlations)[rowSums(species_nutrition_results$p_values <= 0.01 & 
                                                             abs(species_nutrition_results$correlations) >= species_threshold) > 0]
  ))
  
  # Create nodes with custom colors
  ethnicity_nodes <- tibble(
    id = c("C", "I", "M"),
    type = "ethnicity",
    group = "ethnicity",
    color = "darkviolet"  # Gray
  )
  
  food_nodes <- tibble(
    id = food_categories,
    type = "food",
    group = "food",
    color = "#FF6B6B"  # Coral red
  )
  
  nutrition_nodes <- tibble(
    id = nutrition_categories,
    type = "nutrition", 
    group = "nutrition",
    color = "#4ECDC4"  # Turquoise
  )
  
  species_nodes <- tibble(
    id = species_with_connections,
    type = "species",
    group = "species",
    color = "#95A5A6"  # Light gray
  )
  
  # Combine nodes
  nodes <- bind_rows(
    ethnicity_nodes,
    food_nodes,
    nutrition_nodes,
    species_nodes
  )
  
  # Create edges
  species_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_food_results, "species_food")
  species_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_nutrition_results, "species_nutrition")
  
  edges <- bind_rows(
    species_food_edges,
    species_nutrition_edges
  ) %>%
    mutate(correlation_direction = ifelse(weight > 0, "positive", "negative"))
  
  # Create graph
  graph <- tbl_graph(nodes = nodes, edges = edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
    activate(edges) %>%
    mutate(abs_weight = abs(weight))
  
  # Create visualization with custom node colors
  p <- ggraph(graph, layout = "fr", weights = abs_weight) +
    geom_edge_link(aes(color = correlation_direction, 
                      width = abs(weight),
                      alpha = abs(weight))) +
    geom_node_point(aes(color = color), size = 4) +
    geom_node_text(aes(label = id, 
                   size = group),  # Map size to node group
               repel = TRUE) +
    scale_size_manual(values = c(
      "food" = 6.5,        # Larger font for food nodes
      "nutrition" = 6.5,   # Medium font for nutrition nodes
      "species" = 4,     # Smaller font for species nodes
      "ethnicity" = 6.5    # Larger font for ethnicity nodes
      )) +
    scale_edge_width_continuous(range = c(0.2, 2)) +
    scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("positive" = "salmon2", "negative" = "steelblue2")) +
    scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.2, 0.8)) +
    scale_color_identity() +  # Use the actual colors specified in nodes
    theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
    labs(title = "Microbiome Knowledge Graph",
         subtitle = paste("Significant connections (p < 0.05, |correlation| >=", species_threshold, ") between\n",
                         length(species_with_connections), 
                         "species and food/nutrition variables\n",
                         "Edge width represents correlation strength\n",
                         "Red = positive correlation, Blue = negative correlation")) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.box = "vertical",
      plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20)
    )
  
  return(list(
    graph = graph,
    plot = p,
    nodes = nodes,
    edges = edges,
    species_with_connections = species_with_connections,
    results = list(
      species_food = species_food_results,
      species_nutrition = species_nutrition_results
    )
  ))
}
```


```{r fig.width=14, fig.height=14}
# result <- create_microbiome_knowledge_graph(food_data, nutrition_data, 
#                                           species_filtered1, metadata)

result = create_microbiome_knowledge_graph(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                                species_filtered1, metadata,
                                                species_threshold = 0.1)
# View the plot
print(result$plot)

# Save the plot
#ggsave("figure/microbiome_graph3.pdf", result$plot, width = 24, height = 20)

# Check correlation summary
cat("\nNumber of significant correlations:", nrow(result$edges))
cat("\nCorrelation strength summary:\n")
print(summary(abs(result$edges$weight)))
```





```{r}

create_microbiome_knowledge_graph <- function(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                            species_data, metadata,
                                            species_threshold = 0.3) {  # Add threshold parameter
  # Ensure all datasets have the same participants and in the same order
  common_participants <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    food_data$Participant_ID,
    nutrition_data$Participant_ID,
    species_data$Participant_ID,
    metadata$Participant_ID
  ))
  
  # Filter and arrange all datasets
  food_data <- food_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  nutrition_data <- nutrition_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  species_data <- species_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  metadata <- metadata %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  # Create dummy variables for ethnicity
  ethnicity_vars <- metadata %>%
    transmute(
      Participant_ID,
      Chinese = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C", 1, 0),
      Inidan = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I", 1, 0),
      Malay = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M", 1, 0)
    )
  
  # Get categories
  food_categories <- setdiff(colnames(food_data), "Participant_ID")
  nutrition_categories <- setdiff(colnames(nutrition_data), "Participant_ID")
  species_categories <- setdiff(colnames(species_data), "Participant_ID")
  ethnicity_categories <- setdiff(colnames(ethnicity_vars), "Participant_ID")
  
  # Correlation function for ethnicity with other data
  calculate_ethnicity_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(mat1, mat2, type = "pearson")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  ethnicity_food_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  ethnicity_nutrition_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  ethnicity_species_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    species_data, species_categories
  )
  
  # Existing correlation calculations
  calculate_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(cbind(mat1, mat2), type = "spearman")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  species_food_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  species_nutrition_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  # Create edges from correlations
  create_correlation_edges <- function(cor_results, relationship_type) {
    cors <- cor_results$correlations
    p_values <- cor_results$p_values
    edges <- which(p_values <= 0.01 & abs(cors) >= species_threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)
    
    if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
      tibble(
        from = rownames(cors)[edges[,1]],
        to = colnames(cors)[edges[,2]],
        relationship = relationship_type,
        weight = cors[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])],
        p_value = p_values[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])]
      )
    } else {
      tibble(from = character(), to = character(), 
             relationship = character(), weight = numeric(),
             p_value = numeric())
    }
  }
  
  # Define all nodes
  ethnicity_nodes <- tibble(
    id = ethnicity_categories,
    type = "ethnicity",
    group = "ethnicity",
    color = "darkviolet"
  )
  
  food_nodes <- tibble(
    id = food_categories,
    type = "food",
    group = "food",
    color = "#FF6B6B"
  )
  
  nutrition_nodes <- tibble(
    id = nutrition_categories,
    type = "nutrition", 
    group = "nutrition",
    color = "#4ECDC4"
  )
  
  species_nodes <- tibble(
    id = species_categories,
    type = "species",
    group = "species",
    color = "#95A5A6"
  )
  
  nodes <- bind_rows(
    ethnicity_nodes,
    food_nodes,
    nutrition_nodes,
    species_nodes
  )
  
  # Create edges
  species_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_food_results, "species_food")
  species_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_nutrition_results, "species_nutrition")
  ethnicity_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_food_results, "ethnicity_food")
  ethnicity_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_nutrition_results, "ethnicity_nutrition")
  ethnicity_species_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_species_results, "ethnicity_species")
  
  #print(ethnicity_food_edges)
  edges <- bind_rows(
    species_food_edges,
    species_nutrition_edges,
    ethnicity_food_edges,
    ethnicity_nutrition_edges,
    ethnicity_species_edges
  ) %>%
    mutate(correlation_direction = ifelse(weight > 0, "positive", "negative"))
  
  all_edge_ids <- unique(c(edges$from, edges$to))
  nodes <- nodes %>%
    filter(id %in% all_edge_ids)
  
  # Create graph
  graph <- tbl_graph(nodes = nodes, edges = edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
    activate(edges) %>%
    mutate(abs_weight = abs(weight))
  
  # Visualization
  #layout <- create_layout(graph, layout = "fr", niter = 1000, weights = abs_weight)
  # layout <- create_layout(graph, layout = "stress")  # Using stress layout instead of fr

# Set fixed positions for ethnicity nodes
#   layout$x[nodes$id == "Chinese"] <- 1
#   layout$x[nodes$id == "Indian"] <- 2
#   layout$x[nodes$id == "Malay"] <- 1.5
#   layout$y[nodes$id == "Chinese"] <- 0
#   layout$y[nodes$id == "Indian"] <- 0
#   layout$y[nodes$id == "Malay"] <- -2
# 
# # Create the visualization
# p <- ggraph(graph, layout = "manual", x = layout$x, y = layout$y) +
#     geom_edge_link(aes(color = correlation_direction, 
#                       width = abs(weight),
#                       alpha = abs(weight))) +
#     geom_node_point(aes(color = color), size = 4) +
#     geom_node_text(aes(label = id, size = group), repel = TRUE) +
#     scale_size_manual(values = c(
#       "food" = 7.5, "nutrition" = 7.5, 
#       "species" = 6, "ethnicity" = 7.5
#     )) +
#     scale_edge_width_continuous(range = c(1, 2)) +
#     scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("positive" = "salmon2", "negative" = "steelblue2")) +
#     scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.4, 0.9)) +
#     scale_color_identity() +
#     theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
#     labs(title = "Microbiome Knowledge Graph") +
#     theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20))
 p <- ggraph(graph, layout = "fr", weights = abs_weight) +
    geom_edge_link(aes(color = correlation_direction, 
                      width = abs(weight),
                      alpha = abs(weight))) +
    geom_node_point(aes(color = color), size = 4) +
    geom_node_text(aes(label = id, size = group), repel = TRUE) +
    scale_size_manual(values = c(
      "food" = 7.5, "nutrition" = 7.5, 
      "species" = 6, "ethnicity" = 7.5
    )) +
    scale_edge_width_continuous(range = c(1, 2)) +
    scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("positive" = "salmon2", "negative" = "steelblue2")) +
    scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.4, 0.9)) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
    labs(title = "Microbiome Knowledge Graph") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20))
  
  return(list(
    graph = graph,
    plot = p,
    nodes = nodes,
    edges = edges,
    results = list(
      species_food = species_food_results,
      species_nutrition = species_nutrition_results,
      ethnicity_food = ethnicity_food_results,
      ethnicity_nutrition = ethnicity_nutrition_results,
      ethnicity_species = ethnicity_species_results
    )
  ))
}

```

```{r}
species_filtered3 = subset(species_filtered1, select = -c(`Homo sapiens`))
#set.seed(456)
result2 = create_microbiome_knowledge_graph(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                                species_filtered3, metadata,
                                                species_threshold = 0.18)
# View the plot

#ggsave("figure/figure5_network8.pdf", result2$plot, width = 24, height = 20)
print(result2$plot)
```
##change node shapes
```{r}

create_microbiome_knowledge_graph3 <- function(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                            species_data, metadata,
                                            species_threshold = 0.3) {  # Add threshold parameter
  # Ensure all datasets have the same participants and in the same order
  common_participants <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    food_data$Participant_ID,
    nutrition_data$Participant_ID,
    species_data$Participant_ID,
    metadata$Participant_ID
  ))
  
  # Filter and arrange all datasets
  food_data <- food_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  nutrition_data <- nutrition_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  species_data <- species_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  metadata <- metadata %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  # Create dummy variables for ethnicity
  ethnicity_vars <- metadata %>%
    transmute(
      Participant_ID,
      Chinese = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C", 1, 0),
      Inidan = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I", 1, 0),
      Malay = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M", 1, 0)
    )
  
  # Get categories
  food_categories <- setdiff(colnames(food_data), "Participant_ID")
  nutrition_categories <- setdiff(colnames(nutrition_data), "Participant_ID")
  species_categories <- setdiff(colnames(species_data), "Participant_ID")
  ethnicity_categories <- setdiff(colnames(ethnicity_vars), "Participant_ID")
  
  # Correlation function for ethnicity with other data
  calculate_ethnicity_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(mat1, mat2, type = "pearson")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  ethnicity_food_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  ethnicity_nutrition_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  ethnicity_species_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    species_data, species_categories
  )
  
  # Existing correlation calculations
  calculate_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(cbind(mat1, mat2), type = "spearman")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  species_food_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  species_nutrition_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  # Create edges from correlations
  create_correlation_edges <- function(cor_results, relationship_type) {
    cors <- cor_results$correlations
    p_values <- cor_results$p_values
    edges <- which(p_values <= 0.01 & abs(cors) >= species_threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)
    
    if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
      tibble(
        from = rownames(cors)[edges[,1]],
        to = colnames(cors)[edges[,2]],
        relationship = relationship_type,
        weight = cors[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])],
        p_value = p_values[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])]
      )
    } else {
      tibble(from = character(), to = character(), 
             relationship = character(), weight = numeric(),
             p_value = numeric())
    }
  }
  
  # Define all nodes
  ethnicity_nodes <- tibble(
    id = ethnicity_categories,
    type = "ethnicity",
    group = "ethnicity",
    color = "#9180AC"
  )
  
  food_nodes <- tibble(
    id = food_categories,
    type = "food",
    group = "food",
    color = "#9DD0C7"
  )
  
  nutrition_nodes <- tibble(
    id = nutrition_categories,
    type = "nutrition", 
    group = "nutrition",
    color = "#D9BDD8"
  )
  
  species_nodes <- tibble(
    id = species_categories,
    type = "species",
    group = "species",
    color = "#95A5A6"
  )
  
  nodes <- bind_rows(
    ethnicity_nodes,
    food_nodes,
    nutrition_nodes,
    species_nodes
  )
  
  # Create edges
  species_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_food_results, "species_food")
  species_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_nutrition_results, "species_nutrition")
  ethnicity_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_food_results, "ethnicity_food")
  ethnicity_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_nutrition_results, "ethnicity_nutrition")
  ethnicity_species_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_species_results, "ethnicity_species")
  
  #print(ethnicity_food_edges)
  edges <- bind_rows(
    species_food_edges,
    species_nutrition_edges,
    ethnicity_food_edges,
    ethnicity_nutrition_edges,
    ethnicity_species_edges
  ) %>%
    mutate(correlation_direction = ifelse(weight > 0, "positive", "negative"))
  
  all_edge_ids <- unique(c(edges$from, edges$to))
  nodes <- nodes %>%
    filter(id %in% all_edge_ids)
  
  # Create graph
  graph <- tbl_graph(nodes = nodes, edges = edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
    activate(edges) %>%
    mutate(abs_weight = abs(weight))
  

  graph <- graph %>%
    activate(nodes) %>%
    mutate(degree = centrality_degree())
  
  # Modify the visualization part
  p <- ggraph(graph, layout = "fr", weights = abs_weight) +
    # Edge visualization remains the same
    geom_edge_link(aes(color = correlation_direction, 
                      width = abs(weight),
                      alpha = abs(weight))) +
    # Node visualization with shapes and size based on degree
    geom_node_point(aes(color = color,
                        shape = group,
                        size = degree)) +
    geom_node_text(aes(label = id), 
                 repel = TRUE,
                 size = 4,    # Smaller text size for all node labels
                 fontface = "plain") +
    # Shape scale for different node types
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(
      "food" = 16,      # solid circle
      "nutrition" = 18, # solid diamond
      "species" = 17,   # solid triangle
      "ethnicity" = 15  # solid square
    )) +
    # Size scale for degree
    scale_size_continuous(
      name = "Node Degree",
      range = c(3, 9)  # Reduced size range
    ) +
    # Other scales remain the same
    scale_edge_width_continuous(range = c(1, 2)) +
    scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("positive" = "salmon2", 
                                      "negative" = "steelblue2")) +
    scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.4, 0.9)) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    # Theme customization
    theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
    labs(title = "Microbiome Knowledge Graph",
         shape = "Node Type",
         size = "Connections") +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
      legend.box = "vertical"
    )
  
  # The rest of the function remains the same
  return(list(
    graph = graph,
    plot = p,
    nodes = nodes,
    edges = edges,
    results = list(
      species_food = species_food_results,
      species_nutrition = species_nutrition_results,
      ethnicity_food = ethnicity_food_results,
      ethnicity_nutrition = ethnicity_nutrition_results,
      ethnicity_species = ethnicity_species_results
    )
  ))
}

```

```{r}
set.seed(17)
species_filtered3 = subset(species_filtered1, select = -c(`Homo sapiens`))
#set.seed(456)
result4 = create_microbiome_knowledge_graph3(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                                species_filtered3, metadata,
                                                species_threshold = 0.16)
# View the plot

#ggsave("figure/figure5_network12.pdf", result4$plot, width = 24, height = 20)
print(result4$plot)
```
# discrete nodes
```{r}
create_microbiome_knowledge_graph4 <- function(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                            species_data, metadata,
                                            species_threshold) {  
  # Ensure all datasets have the same participants and in the same order
  common_participants <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    food_data$Participant_ID,
    nutrition_data$Participant_ID,
    species_data$Participant_ID,
    metadata$Participant_ID
  ))
  
  # Filter and arrange all datasets
  food_data <- food_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  nutrition_data <- nutrition_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  species_data <- species_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  metadata <- metadata %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  # Create dummy variables for ethnicity
  ethnicity_vars <- metadata %>%
    transmute(
      Participant_ID,
      Chinese = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "C", 1, 0),
      Indian = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "I", 1, 0),
      Malay = ifelse(FREG5_Ethnic_Group == "M", 1, 0)
    )
  
  # Get categories
  food_categories <- setdiff(colnames(food_data), "Participant_ID")
  nutrition_categories <- setdiff(colnames(nutrition_data), "Participant_ID")
  species_categories <- setdiff(colnames(species_data), "Participant_ID")
  ethnicity_categories <- setdiff(colnames(ethnicity_vars), "Participant_ID")
  
  # Correlation function for ethnicity with other data
  calculate_ethnicity_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(mat1, mat2, type = "pearson")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  ethnicity_food_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  ethnicity_nutrition_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  ethnicity_species_results <- calculate_ethnicity_correlations(
    ethnicity_vars, ethnicity_categories,
    species_data, species_categories
  )
  
  # Existing correlation calculations
  calculate_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(cbind(mat1, mat2), type = "spearman")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  species_food_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  species_nutrition_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  # Create edges from correlations
  create_correlation_edges <- function(cor_results, relationship_type) {
    cors <- cor_results$correlations
    p_values <- cor_results$p_values
    edges <- which(p_values <= 0.01 & abs(cors) >= species_threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)
    
    if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
      tibble(
        from = rownames(cors)[edges[,1]],
        to = colnames(cors)[edges[,2]],
        relationship = relationship_type,
        weight = cors[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])],
        p_value = p_values[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])]
      )
    } else {
      tibble(from = character(), to = character(), 
             relationship = character(), weight = numeric(),
             p_value = numeric())
    }
  }
  
  # Define all nodes
  ethnicity_nodes <- tibble(
    id = ethnicity_categories,
    type = "ethnicity",
    group = "ethnicity",
    color = "#9180AC",
    text_size = 10
  )
  
  food_nodes <- tibble(
    id = food_categories,
    type = "food",
    group = "food",
    color = "#9DD0C7",
    text_size = 5
  )
  
  nutrition_nodes <- tibble(
    id = nutrition_categories,
    type = "nutrition", 
    group = "nutrition",
    color = "#D9BDD8",
    text_size = 4
  )
  
  species_nodes <- tibble(
    id = species_categories,
    type = "species",
    group = "species",
    color = "#95A5A6",
    text_size = 3
  )
  
  nodes <- bind_rows(
    ethnicity_nodes,
    food_nodes,
    nutrition_nodes,
    species_nodes
  )
  
  # Create edges
  species_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_food_results, "species_food")
  species_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_nutrition_results, "species_nutrition")
  ethnicity_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_food_results, "ethnicity_food")
  ethnicity_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_nutrition_results, "ethnicity_nutrition")
  ethnicity_species_edges <- create_correlation_edges(ethnicity_species_results, "ethnicity_species")
  
  edges <- bind_rows(
    species_food_edges,
    species_nutrition_edges,
    ethnicity_food_edges,
    ethnicity_nutrition_edges,
    ethnicity_species_edges
  ) %>%
    mutate(correlation_direction = ifelse(weight > 0, "positive", "negative"))
  
  all_edge_ids <- unique(c(edges$from, edges$to))
  nodes <- nodes %>%
    filter(id %in% all_edge_ids)
  
  # Create graph
  graph <- tbl_graph(nodes = nodes, edges = edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
    activate(edges) %>%
    mutate(abs_weight = abs(weight))
  
  graph <- graph %>%
    activate(nodes) %>%
    mutate(degree = centrality_degree())
  
  # Modified visualization part with more discrete nodes
  p <- ggraph(graph, layout = "fr", weights = abs_weight) +
    # Edge visualization with reduced prominence
    geom_edge_link(aes(color = correlation_direction, 
                      width = abs(weight),
                      alpha = abs(weight)),
                   alpha = 0.8) +  # Reduced overall edge opacity
    # More discrete node visualization
    geom_node_point(aes(color = color,
                       shape = group,
                       size = degree),
                   stroke = 1.2,        # Increased border thickness
                   alpha = 1) +       # Slightly transparent nodes
    geom_node_text(aes(label = id, size = text_size), 
                 repel = TRUE,
                 fontface = "plain",
                 vjust = 2,            # Increased label distance
                 segment.size = 0.2,    # Thinner repel lines
                 max.overlaps = 20) +
    # Modified shape scale for different node types
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(
      "food" = 16,      # hollow circle
      "nutrition" = 23, # hollow diamond
      "species" = 24,   # hollow triangle
      "ethnicity" = 15  # hollow square
    )) +
    # More discrete size scale
    scale_size_continuous(
      name = "Node Degree",
      range = c(3, 12),    # Reduced size range
      breaks = function(x) pretty(x, n = 4)
    ) +
    # Adjusted scales for more discrete appearance
    scale_edge_width_continuous(range = c(0.3, 1.5)) + # Thinner edges
    scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("positive" = alpha("salmon2", 0.6), 
                                      "negative" = alpha("steelblue2", 0.6))) +
    scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.8, 1)) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    # Theme customization
    theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
    labs(title = "Microbiome Knowledge Graph",
         shape = "Node Type",
         size = "Connections") +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
      legend.box = "vertical",
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
    )
  
  return(list(
    graph = graph,
    plot = p,
    nodes = nodes,
    edges = edges,
    results = list(
      species_food = species_food_results,
      species_nutrition = species_nutrition_results,
      ethnicity_food = ethnicity_food_results,
      ethnicity_nutrition = ethnicity_nutrition_results,
      ethnicity_species = ethnicity_species_results
    )
  ))
}
```

```{r}

```


```{r}
#1717 717
set.seed(124)
species_filtered3 = subset(species_filtered1, select = -c(`Homo sapiens`))
#set.seed(456)
result5 = create_microbiome_knowledge_graph4(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                                species_filtered3, metadata,
                                                species_threshold = 0.16)
# View the plot

ggsave("figure/figure5_network15.pdf", result5$plot, width = 24, height = 20)
print(result5$plot)
```
```{r}
food_data
```

```{r}
correlation_matrices <- list(
  species_food = result5$results$species_food$correlations,
  species_food_pvalues = result5$results$species_food$p_values,
  species_nutrition = result5$results$species_nutrition$correlations,
  species_nutrition_pvalues = result5$results$species_nutrition$p_values,
  ethnicity_food = result5$results$ethnicity_food$correlations,
  ethnicity_food_pvalues = result5$results$ethnicity_food$p_values,
  ethnicity_nutrition = result5$results$ethnicity_nutrition$correlations,
  ethnicity_nutrition_pvalues = result5$results$ethnicity_nutrition$p_values,
  ethnicity_species = result5$results$ethnicity_species$correlations,
  ethnicity_species_pvalues = result5$results$ethnicity_species$p_values
)

# Convert each matrix to a data frame with rownames as a column
correlation_dfs <- lapply(correlation_matrices, function(mat) {
  df <- as.data.frame(mat)
  df <- cbind(rowname = rownames(df), df)
  return(df)
})

correlation_dfs
#write_xlsx(correlation_dfs, "data/microbiome_correlations.xlsx")
```
